topic: My mailbox keeps getting letters addressed to people who never lived at my address
title: Letters In My Mailbox Are Addressed To People Who Never Lived Here | True Scary Story

scenes.narration: There were three letters in my mailbox yesterday. None of those names had ever lived at my address.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of three yellowed envelopes resting inside a metal apartment mailbox, dim hallway light behind, the apartment number panel partially visible, dust along the envelope edges, low angle composition, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I have rented the same apartment for eleven years.
scenes.image_prompt: long view down an empty apartment hallway at dusk, identical brown doors lining both walls, a mailbox bank at the near end, single fluorescent strip humming overhead, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I know every neighbor on my floor.
scenes.image_prompt: a row of brass apartment mailboxes labeled with handwritten name cards, slightly dusty, late evening light from a window at the end of the lobby, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: The first letter was addressed to a woman. The postmark was nineteen sixty seven.
scenes.image_prompt: a single weathered envelope held by a hand, brown paper, faded blue ink address, partial round postmark visible in the corner, dim apartment hallway out of focus behind, vertical 9:16 framing
a man's hand frozen above an open envelope on a kitchen table, late evening lamp light, the rest of the kitchen in shadow, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Then I read the next two. Same handwriting. Different decades.
scenes.image_prompt: two more envelopes laid side by side on a wooden kitchen table, both showing the same handwriting in different ink colors, ambient lamp light, vertical 9:16 framing
a corded phone receiver held against an ear, kitchen wall behind with faded floral wallpaper, late evening lamp light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: They had no record of any of them.
scenes.image_prompt: closeup of a hand pressing the disconnect button on a cordless phone resting on a kitchen counter, dim ambient light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: That night I checked my mailbox again. There were four more.
scenes.image_prompt: open metal apartment mailbox at night, multiple envelopes packed inside, porch light dimly illuminating the slot, brick wall behind, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: The newest letter was addressed to me. The postmark read two thousand and forty one.
scenes.image_prompt: closeup of an envelope addressed in faded ink with a round postmark stamped in red, held in a slightly trembling hand, dim kitchen lighting, vertical 9:16 framing
an unopened envelope sitting alone on a wooden side table, single lamp casting an amber pool of light, the rest of the room dark, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: My mailbox has been locked for nine days. The letters keep arriving anyway.
scenes.image_prompt: a padlocked metal mailbox at night, a thin pile of envelopes visibly jammed into the slot, porch light off, only streetlight illumination, vertical 9:16 framing
